mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 65 percent of periods following a federal funds rate increase.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack accelerates, reducing consumer spending power and corporate profit growth, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 70 percent of instances where the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, forcing earnings downgrades and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 20-day returns when both retail sales and housing starts decline month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports near-term equity momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of months where the industrial production index rises.

mechanism_narrative: Tight monetary policy combined with rising unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that severely compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in greater than 55 percent of periods where both the funds rate and unemployment rate exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates severe economic deceleration that triggers recession fears, causing institutional portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of quarters where GDP growth drops below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor demand and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of months following a nonfarm payroll increase.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, eroding purchasing power and prompting central bank tightening that ultimately suppresses equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of months where the CPI index exceeds the federal funds rate.
